mnemonics.title: MODUS PONENS记忆
mnemonics.content: P for First: (P → Q) and P, so Q follows. Like 'Pressing Power button (P) turns on computer (Q)'

mnemonics.title: MODUS TOLLENS记忆
mnemonics.content: T for Terminate: Negate the conclusion to terminate the antecedent. Like 'No smoke (¬Q) means no fire (¬P)'

mnemonics.title: HYPOTHETICAL SYLLOGISM记忆
mnemonics.content: Chain reactions: A→B, B→C, so A→C. Like 'If you study (A), you pass (B); if you pass (B), you graduate (C)'

acronyms.usage: From (p→q) and p, conclude q. Used in direct proofs.
From (p→q) and ¬q, conclude ¬p. Used in contradiction proofs.
From (p∨q) and ¬p, conclude q. Used in case analysis.
From p, conclude (p∨q). Always safe to add options.
From (p∧q), conclude p. Break complex conjunctions.

visualTips.visualization: Think of p→q as a promise: broken only when promise (p=true) kept but result (q) fails. F T F is the only false case.
Swap positions AND negate both: Like 'mirror + flip'. The most reliable equivalence.
Like a water pipe: if water (truth) flows in from all premises, it MUST flow out from conclusion.

analogies.analogy: Like a cause and effect: Switch ON (p), light turns ON (q). Switch is ON, so light must be ON.
Like detective work: If thief entered (p), door is broken (q). Door is NOT broken (¬q), so thief did NOT enter (¬p).
A mathematically correct formula that doesn't describe reality. Like 'All cats are dogs' (valid form but false premise).
